observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation and the management agreed to (a) facilitate the conduct of a thorough physical count of all PPE items and the proper preparation of RPCPPE; (b) require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer/Supply Officer to maintain PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, in accordance with the format prescribed by MNGAS and ensure reconciliation of these PPE records; and (c) require the Acting Municipal Accountant to (i) immediately record the unrecognized parcel of land owned by the Municipality; (ii) recognize in the books the slope protection constructed in CY 2017 under Flood Control System, if warranted; and (iii) record all necessary adjusting entries pertaining to erroneous recording of depreciation of Land Improvement, Parks, Plazas and Monuments and Watercrafts accounts. Moreover, we recommended and the Management agreed to consider the one time cleansing of PPE accounts provided under COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 to finally be able to reflect a realistic and reliable balances of the PPE accounts.
observations.managementAction:
The concerned department heads already convene a meeting in relation to the conduct of physical count of PPE and will be scheduled by November up to December. As to the necessary documentations needed, the concerned departments already starting to plot all these documents like PPE ledger card with the Accounting Department and supply officer. Rest assured that those documents will be available by year end.
Unrecognized PPE. The unrecorded parcel of land with OCT No. 2152 that was located at Sta. Cruz Poblacion was already recorded on the February 2024 reports (JEV-GENERAL FUND). As to the error on recording the depreciation expense of certain PPE from previous years, upon verification, were already adjusted by recording the necessary adjusting entries per property accounts on March 2024 reports (JEV-GENERAL FUND).

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation and the Management agreed to require: (a) the MTO (all concerned department heads and designated supply officers, as the case may be) to (i) prepare and maintain stock cards for each type of supply/inventory; and (ii) ensure the preparation and timely submission of Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) and Requisition Issue Slip (RSI) to the Accounting Office; (b) the Accounting Office to (i) prepare and maintain supplies ledger cards (SLC) for each type of supply/inventory; and (ii) religiously reconcile this with the stock cards to be maintained by the MTO or concerned department/unit; (c) the Inventory Committee to (i) conduct the periodic physical count of all inventories in order to establish the correct balances of the inventory accounts; and (ii) prepare and submit the required Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) resulting therefrom. Also, recommended to consider establishing a central storage facility to accommodate regular inventory purchases and improve monitoring of inventory receipt and issuance, in accordance with the perpetual inventory system.
observations.managementAction: The concerned department heads already convene a meeting in relation to the conduct of inventory and will be scheduled by November up to December. As to the necessary documents needed, the concerned departments already starting to plot all these documents like Supplies ledger card with the accounting department and supply officer. Rest assured that those documents will be available by year end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to instruct: (a) the MAO and MTO to reconcile their records for business taxes and ensure that the recorded income is equal to the collected amounts for business taxes; (b) the BPLO to (i) strictly based its assessment on the submitted tax returns and sworn declaration of taxpayers and/or their authorized and approximate the gross sales/receipts through the use of an authorized stratified presumptive income level ONLY when taxpayers are not able to provide tax returns or sworn declarations; (ii) require the submission of audited FS and/or ITR for CY 2023 for re-assessment of CY 2024 LBTs and collect any tax deficiency arising from re-assessment, if any; (iii) re-calculate the tax deficiencies determined by the Audit Team and consider collecting the same from the taxpayers, if warranted; and (iv) henceforth, require the submission of audited FS and/or ITR of the taxpayers on or before April 30 each year and ensure the correctness of tax base used, the tax assessments and amount collected for LBT and; (c) consider increasing the staff at the Business Permit and Licensing Office (BPLO), provided that it aligns with the limitations on Personal Services. This would enhance the BPLO's capacity to efficiently manage business permits and licensing processes. We also recommended and the Management agreed that the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) consider the revision of the Municipal Revenue Code in order to maximize the revenue-generating powers of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Rest assured that the Municipality will continuously comply with all the requirements in relation to the utilization of all GAD Projects. The office presented the stratified schedule of presumptive income level (basis on the revision of the municipal revenue code) to the office of the Sangguniang Bayan. The body is now reviewing the proposal.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive and MENRO (a) encourage active participation of barangay officials to support the solid waste management program of the LGU by educating their constituents on the proper segregation of reusable or recyclable materials from compostable wastes and to the establishment their own MRF through allocating funds to put up eight MRFs in all the Barangays of San Luis; (b) to put in writing or agreement the terms and conditions of partnership in diversion of waste with selected parties; and (c) the LCE require the MENRO to install effective measures for proper compliance of barangays to Republic Act 9003.
observations.managementAction: The lacking documents that are sighted in the audit observation were already submitted during exit conference. The LGU assures that all claims from government funds are supported with complete documentation.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations and the Management agreed to instruct the Acting Municipal Accountant to (a) strictly conduct monthly reconciliation between the recognized collected taxes, contributions/premiums and loan amortization payments and the remittances made; and (b) investigate the cause of outstanding balances of the Due to BIR, GSIS, HDMF and PhilHealth to determine if these refer to unremitted balances in the prior years and immediately remit the same if so warranted.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation of premiums, and loans against the remittances made is being done. Close coordination with the GSIS is being undertaken to determine the final balance of the unremitted premiums as well as HDMF, PhilHealth and BIR.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year’s recommendations and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the (a) Municipal Accountant to (i) promptly review and effect the necessary adjustments to correct the recorded cash balance in the financial statement to reflect accurate and reconciled amounts; (ii) immediately record all valid reconciling items; and (iii) explore alternative solutions to resolve long-outstanding reconciling items when all efforts to locate supporting documents have been exhausted; and (b) Municipal Treasurer to immediately send the supporting documents for the unreported disbursements to the Accounting Office for proper recording.

We reaffirmed our prior year’s recommendation and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to take decisive actions in addressing outstanding cash advances. Specifically, this includes: (a) actively monitoring and ensuring the follow-through of final demand letters issued to those responsible for liquidation; (b) continuing the withholding of salaries in cases where liquidation is unjustifiably delayed; (c) recommending the filing of appropriate administrative or criminal charges against the accountable officers, if warranted; (d) strictly enforcing the policy of denying additional cash advances until prior ones have been settled; and (e) reinforcing and effectively implementing the internal control system governing the granting and liquidation of cash advances.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the (a) Inventory Team to complete the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances, investigate and adjust the discrepancies in PPE account balances accordingly; (b) Municipal Accountant to investigate and reconcile the unreconciled Depreciation Expense; (c) ensure the prompt application for insurance coverage with GSIS for all insurable buildings and motor vehicles; and (d) Management to hire permanent employee who will handle all the property management of the municipality.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal Accountant to (i) adjust the CIP balance consistent with the actual status of the identified project; (ii) recognize the corresponding depreciation costs computed from the time the PPE were made available for use in accordance with paragraph 3 of IPSAS 17; and (iii) henceforth regularly coordinate with the MEO for the updated status of the projects included in the CIP account; and (b) Municipal Engineer to (i) furnish the Municipal Accountant with the updated status of the projects which should clearly indicate projects that were already completed; and (ii) henceforth provide a quarterly status report of ongoing projects.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed through the (a) Municipal Treasurer to (i) introduce collection strategy that would significantly help increase the revenue from RPT/SET Receivables of the Municipality ; (ii) prepare an updated Certified List of Taxpayers including delinquents, detailing property particulars, tax due and other relevant information and submit the same to the Accounting Office as basis in setting up RPT/SET Receivables in the books of accounts of the Municipality at the beginning of the year; and (iii) ensure that the procedures in setting up RPT/SET complies with the provisions of Section 20 of MNGAS; and (b) Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to reconcile and adjust records on RPT/SET Receivables to reflect accurate balances in the books of the Municipality.

We reiterated our recommendation and the LCE agreed to (a) require the Municipal Accountant to (i) determine the proper disposition of negative account balances; (ii) analyze the difference of ₱116,069 for the fair representation of the account balance in the financial statements and maintain complete SLs whenever necessary pursuant to Section 111 of PD No. 1445; and (iii) initiate proceedings for the return of unexpended balances of fund transfers, as warranted, and ensure remittance of the BFP share in inspection fees; and (b) make representation with source agencies for the release of the remaining project fund balances.
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed through the Municipal Treasurer to sustain its satisfactory performance in collecting business taxes and conducting regular business tax mapping.

2.7 We recommended and the management agreed, in coordination with the
Municipal Accountant, to: (a) ensure that a cash advance is granted only after the
liquidation of the previous one by requiring certification from the latter confirming
its settlement/accounting before releasing the new one; (b) designate additional SDO
to prevent the granting of multiple CAs to a single SDO; and (c) ensure strict
adherence to applicable rules and regulations governing cash advances.

3.26 We reiterated our recommendation and the management agreed (a) through
the Inventory Committee, facilitate the conduct of a thorough physical count of all
PPE items and the proper preparation of RPCPPE; (b) require the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer/Supply Officer to maintain PPE Ledger Cards
and Property Cards, respectively, in accordance with the format prescribed by
MNGAS and ensure reconciliation of these PPE records.

3.27 After ensuring the integrity of the PPE reports and records through proper
reconciliation and thorough conduct of physical count, we recommended and the
management agreed to undertake the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts as provided
under COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020. This will ultimately allow
for the recognition of more accurate, realistic, and reliable balances in the PPE
accounts.

4.9 We recommended and the management agreed to require the Provincial
Accountant to ensure that all ongoing infrastructure projects are accurately recorded
as CIP in the FS at year-end and to draw the JEVs taking up the costs of the on-going
infrastructure projects based on actual accomplishments.

5.36 We reiterated our recommendation and the management agreed to require:
(a) the MTO (all concerned department heads and designated supply officers, as the
case may be) to (i) prepare and maintain stock cards for each type of
supply/inventory; and (ii) ensure the preparation and timely submission of Summary
of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) and Requisition Issue Slip (RSI) to the
Accounting Office; (b) the Accounting Office to (i) prepare and maintain supplies
ledger cards (SLC) for each type of supply/inventory; and (ii) religiously reconcile
this with the stock cards to be maintained by the MTO or concerned department/unit;
and (c) the Inventory Committee to (i) conduct a comprehensive periodic physical
count of ALL inventories in order to establish the correct balances of the inventory
accounts; and (ii) ensure the correct preparation of the Report on the Physical Count
of Inventories (RPCI) resulting therefrom and submit the same to the COA Office
within the prescribed period

5.37 We also recommended and the management agreed to consider establishing a
central storage facility to accommodate regular inventory purchases and improve
monitoring of inventory receipt and issuance, in accordance with the perpetual
inventory system.

5.38 Lastly, we recommended and the Accounting Office agreed to make all the
necessary adjusting entries to correct the recording errors discussed in the
observations.

6.8 We reiterated our recommendation and the management agreed to require the
Municipal Accounting Office to (a) continue to exert best efforts to trace, analyze and
reconcile the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts and make the
necessary adjustments to reflect the balances of the said accounts and furnish the
COA the corrections made; and (b) henceforth, prepare and maintain the necessary
subsidiary records supporting the balances of the said reciprocal accounts.

7.15 We recommended and the management agreed to require the Accounting
Office to make all necessary adjustments to correct the balances of the affected
accounts and henceforth follow all the accounting procedures set forth in the NGAS
Manual for LGUs pertaining to setting-up, collection and distribution of Real
Property Tax and Special Education Tax. The Accounting Office may start by setting
up the correct RPT/SET Receivables and Deferred RPT/SET Income at the beginning
of CY 2025 based on the Certified List of Real Property Tax Delinquencies as of
December 31, 2024 and set up the correct amount of additional collectibles for CY
2025 based on the Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s index card.

8.6 We recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal
Accountant: (a) include supporting evidence and traces of records of the transactions
under Prior Period Adjustments in the entries recorded in Cash Disbursement
Journals and Journal Entry Vouchers, such as original entry, to substantiate the
adjustments; and (b) reflect disclosure of Prior Period Adjustments in the Notes to
Financial Statements in accordance with International Public Sector Accounting
Standards 3 or Accounting Policies, Changes in Accounting Estimates and Errors.

Considering the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. instruct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible before yearend for each tax period and furnish the same to the Municipal Accountant for proper recognition of RPT Receivables and SET Receivables in the books of accounts;
b. direct the Municipal Accountant to review the accuracy of the balances of RPT Receivables, SET Receivables, Deferred RPT, and Deferred SET accounts in the books of accounts and trace the reason behind the aforementioned discrepancies; and
c. prepare and record the necessary adjusting entries to rectify the understatement/overstatement on the RPT Receivables, SET Receivables, Deferred RPT, and Deferred SET accounts and ensure compliance with proper recording of RPT/SET collections for the ensuing years.

a. comply strictly with the disclosure requirements in the preparation of the Notes to Financial Statements, particularly the disclosures for Other Receipts, Other Expenses, Available Fund and Utilization of the LDRRM Fund, Reconciliation of Net Cash Flows from Operating Activities to Surplus, and Reconciliation between SCBAA and the SFPer, and submit the same to the Audit Team for further evaluation and analysis; and
b. for the succeeding year, prepare the NFS using the prescribed Pro-forma NFS. Ensure that each item on the face of the statement of financial position, statement of financial performance, statement of changes in net assets/equity and cash flow statement shall be cross-referenced to any related information in the notes thus address any deficiency and present reliable and accurate information to the public.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. require the GFPS Executive Committee (Execom) to submit the Annual GPB to the Provincial Planning and Development Office (PPDO), from PPDO to the DILG Provincial Office and secure the Certificate of Endorsement from the DILG Provincial Office, where such certification would truly establish the relevance of the gender issues identified and gender-responsiveness of the PPAs indicated in the GPB pursuant to Section 5.0 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01; and
b. require the GFPS to ensure that the GPB submitted to the DILG is the final and approved version, and that its contents—particularly the PPAs and corresponding budget—are consistent with those reflected in the Annual Budget and the GAD Accomplishment Report to avoid discrepancies.

a. submit the Quarterly and Annual SEF Utilization Reports for CY 2024, and in succeeding years, to the DepEd Central Office, through appropriate channels, and furnish copies thereof to the Sangguniang Bayan and Regional Offices of the DBM and DILG pursuant to Item 6.1 of the DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1 s. 2017, to ensure transparency and accountability in reporting the allocation and utilization of the Special Education Fund to the intended users of information; and

Executive (LCE) agreed to require the (a) Municipal Accountant to (i)
immediately record all valid reconciling items; and (ii) explore other options to
clear the long outstanding reconciling items when all efforts to locate the
supporting documents has been exhausted; and (b) Municipal Treasurer to
immediately forward to the Accounting Office the supporting documents for the
unreported disbursement for proper recording.

2.18. We reiterated our recommendations and the LCE agreed to instruct the
Municipal Accountant to (a) aggressively monitor and follow through the final
demand letters issued to the persons responsible for the liquidation of the
outstanding cash advances; (b) continue the withholding of salaries in case of
unjustified failure to do so; (c) recommend for the filing of appropriate
administrative or criminal charges, as warranted; (d) refrain from granting
additional cash advances if the previous ones remain unsettled; and (e)
strengthen and implement its internal control system on the grant and liquidation of
cash advances.

3.34 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal
Accountant to (i) prepare the appropriate adjusting entries to derecognize the
demolished properties; and (ii) reclassify the two mini dump trucks to their
appropriate account; (b) Municipal Treasurer to (i) prepare the RPCPPE in
conformity with the format and contents prescribed by Annex 49, Chapter II of
MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II and submit to the Audit Team on or before January
31, of each year; (ii) secure all documents pertaining to the registration and
insurance of buildings and motor vehicles; and (iii)submit IIRUP for the
derecognition of demolished properties; (c) Chairman of the Disposal Committee
to facilitate disposal of unserviceable PPE in accordance with the prescribed
guidelines and procedures; and (d) Municipal Treasurer and Municipal
Accountant to strictly adhere to Section 122 of PD 1445 on the submission of
required documents and reports.

4.12 We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the (a) Municipal
Engineer to initiate actions against the erring contractors for defaulting on their
contractual obligations; (b) Municipal Accountant to (i) adjust the erroneous
capitalization of the cost of consulting services pertaining to the Road Route
Survey for the Farm to Market Road - Real – Dibalo through the following entry:
Prior Period Adjustment ₱510,600.00
Construction in Progress -
Infrastructures Assets ₱510,600.00
To adjust the erroneous capitalization of the cost of consulting services
pertaining to the Road Route Survey for the Farm to Market Road -
Real – Dibalo
(ii) coordinate with the Municipal Engineering Office for the quarterly submission
of all documents related to the actual status of projects included in the
Construction in Progress account, and adjust the account balance, as warranted;
and (iii) henceforth regularly monitor the validity of the CIP balance every
reporting period.

5.15 We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) require the Municipal
Accountant to (i) initiate proceedings for the return of unexpended balances of
fund transfers, as warranted, and remittance of the BFP share in inspection fees;
(ii) analyze the difference of ₱116,068.68 for the fair representation of the account
balance in the financial statements and maintain complete SLs whenever necessary
pursuant to Section 111 of PD No. 1445; and (iii) determine the proper disposition
of negative account balances; and (b) make representation with source agencies for

Municipal Engineer to (a) institute more stringent compliance and oversight
mechanisms in the project concreting of road at Brgy. Dikapinisan; (b) henceforth,
ensure that the contractual terms are observed in future transactions; and (c)
sustain the overall commendable performance demonstrated in partnering on the
implementation of national government projects.

Planning and Development Coordinator Office and Municipal Engineer to ensure
that the PPAs to be included in the 20% Development Plan are procurement-and-

and; (b) Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to discontinue

of program implementation, and taking responsive actions on noted gaps; and (b)
work with Municipal Accounting Office for accurate recording of DRRM funds

1.10. We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the (a)Municipal
Accountant to periodically coordinate with the MTO regarding the existence of stale

become stale checks. The Municipal Accountant is likewise advised to take up the

conduct regular reconciliation of the accounts to establish the correctness of both

2.9. We recommended and the Management agreed to instruct the Municipal

3.7. We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation and the management
agreed to (a) facilitate the conduct of a thorough physical count of all PPE items and

adjustments to reflect the balances of the said accounts and furnish the COA the

Based on the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a. Create the Inventory Committee for the one-time cleansing responsible for the conduct of the actual count of the PPEs;

b. Instruct the duly created Inventory Committee, in coordination with Municipal Accountant and MGSO to plan/strategize on how would the Municipality apply the one-time cleansing and incorporate it in the PIP to be submitted at least 10 calendar days before the scheduled inventory-taking activities; and

c. Immediately start the application of one-time cleansing of the PPEs.

In view thereof, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustment/reclassification for each item with cost below the capitalization threshold of P50,000.00 or those considered as Semi-expendable property following COA Circular No. 2022-004 to reflect fairly the accounts balances in the Municipality’s financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to:

a. Recognize appropriate asset, liability, revenue and expense accounts in the Trust Fund books upon receipt and utilization of funds from other agencies arising from non-exchange transactions based on the Revised Chart of Accounts; and

b. Cause the preparation and submission of the SFPer of the Trust Fund along with the other financial statements of the Municipality.

Pursuant to IPSAS No. 1 and COA Circular No. 2016-004, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:

a. Prepare the Reconciliation between the Actual Amounts as presented in the SCBAA and the SFPer as additional disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements to clarify the discrepancies between the two reports, and submit the same to the Audit Team within 15 days from receipt hereof; and

b. For the succeeding year, prepare the NFS using the prescribed Pro-forma NFS. Ensure that each item on the face of the statement of financial position, statement of financial performance, statement of changes in net assets/equity and cash flow statement shall be cross-referenced to any related information in the notes.

Foregoing considered, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:

a. Draw the necessary adjusting entries to correct the misstatements; and

b. Accrue at the end of each succeeding year the transfer of the unexpended QRF and Mitigation Fund- MOOE to Special Trust Fund.

Based on the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MDRRMO to:

a. Submit the Municipality’s plan for the implementation of the unutilized balance of LDRRMF amounting to P5,093,606.83;

b. Submit written justifications and explanations regarding the above observation on unutilized LDRRMF; and

c. Judiciously plan on how to maximize the utilization of the LDRRMF and to accelerate their implementation to achieve the goal/target of the municipality regarding disaster relief, recovery, and risk reduction and management.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GFPS TWG to strictly comply with the provisions on the preparation of the GAD Agenda to ensure that the PPAs indicated in the annual GPB are aligned with it or with the identified gender issues to be addressed during the three-year term of the LCE’s leadership.

Based on the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor as Local Chief Executive, instruct the Chairperson of GFPS-TWG to:

a. Review the GPB thoroughly, submit any changes therein to the DILG-Regional Office, and strictly monitor the actual expenditures to ensure that the Municipality has enough funds for the implementation of all GAD activities; and

b. Submit the GPB within the prescribed period to enable timely review and endorsement of the PPDO and DILG.

Hence, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:

a. The Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate funds for property insurance of the Municipality subject to its financial capacity; and

b. The MGSO to prepare and submit the consolidated PIF listing of all the insurable properties and other assets to GSIS for their evaluation and approval, copy furnish the Office of the Auditor, in compliance to Section 5 of RA No. 656 and COA Circular 2018-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the HRMO Office-Designate to strengthen the system of control in the procurement of personnel under JO, viz:

a. Establish standard policies and procedures in the procurement/hiring of JO employees;

b. Require the applicants to submit valid proofs of identity and suitability for the job being applied for; and

c. Provide a Statement of Duties and Responsibilities to employees to understand their duties and perform efficiently.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:

a. Identify the items that could have caused the variances between the amount withheld and remitted to the concerned agencies;

b. Draw Journal Entry Voucher to make necessary adjusting entries; and

c. In case of identified unremitted contributions, immediately remit the amount to the concerned agency.

which were already paid, otherwise the transactions will be disallowed in
audit;

signatories to faithfully ensure that the materials were actually inspected,

c. The Municipal Engineer or its Inspection Committee to inspect diligently
the status of implementation of the projects before certifying in the
Statement of Work Accomplished (SWA), otherwise be responsible to the
consequences of falsely certifying on a document that becomes a public
document later on; and

d. The Municipal Accountant to adjust the Construction in Progress-
Buildings and Other Structures account considering the noted
deficiencies.

a. Restitution of the ₱53,953,051.93 paid to unappropriated and
ineligible expenditures;

b. Municipal Accountant to assess the recorded Accounts Payable to

account to include only payables with cash back-up;

c. Municipal Budget Officer to refrain from obligating expenditures not
chargeable to the budget of Peace and Order Program and ascertain
that all disbursements/obligations should be within its
appropriation; and

d. Municipal Budget Officer to ascertain that all expenditures and
obligations are properly charge to its appropriate budget allotment.

reconcile the reciprocal accounts, Due from/to Other Funds and Due
from/to Special Accounts, and effect adjustments to reflect the correct
account balances and present reliable data pursuant to Section 111(2) of

Account Title Debit Credit

6.10 We recommended and the Management agreed to adopt the Automated
Teller Machine (ATM) Payroll System in the payment of salaries, wages,
allowances and other personnel benefits instead of granting huge amount
of cash advance to the Disbursing Officer because it promotes

a. The Municipal Treasurer to submit the monthly DVs to the

a. Human Resource Management Officer refrain from (1) approving
monetization of leave credits exceeding the maximum of thirty (30)
days and (2) allowing the monetization of sick leave credits in

terminal leave benefits to preclude the transaction from being suspended
or disallowed. Henceforth, ensure that the payment of terminal leave

to COA Circular No. 2012-001.

10.14 We recommended and the Municipal Engineer agreed to:

a. Undertake proper and careful planning to ensure full implementation

observations.reason: Not Implemented (Reiterated in Part II of this Report)

observations.recommendation:
We commended the Management for (a) complying with the provisions of Republic Act No. 10121 on the proper allocation of the required 5% LDRRMF; and (b) for its prompt and effective efforts exerted towards its constituents in its relief operations conducted specifically during the ECQ and the onslaught of Typhoon Ulysses.

However, we recommended and the Management agreed to:

(a) increase its efforts in maximizing the use of the LDRRMF, both current and prior years, by targeting full-implementation of programmed PPAs to expand its capability and responsiveness in addressing disaster risk reduction and management and climate change adversities; 

(b) strictly comply with the accounting guidelines for LDRRMF pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012, specifically on the transfer of unexpended LDRRMF from Trust Fund to General Fund.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Accounting Department:

(a) conduct monthly reconciliation between the recognized collected premiums and loan amortization payments and the remittances made; and 

(b) investigate the prior year
balances and determine if these are valid obligations of the Municipality.

2.8 We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the reciprocal accounts, Due from/to Other Funds and Due from/to Special Accounts and effect adjustments to reflect the correct account balances and present reliable data pursuant to Section 111(2) of Presidential Decree No. 1445.
The Management duly noted and agreed to the audit recommendation. The Municipal Accountant is still on the process of reconciling the said accounts and assured the Audit Team that necessary adjustments will be effected to correct the account balances.

5.9 We recommended that Management direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the lacking disbursement vouchers and to observe the prescribed period on the submission of DVs to COA. Likewise, the Accountant is advised to refrain from processing of claims that are incomplete of supporting documents.
5.10 The audit recommendation was well taken by the Management and will strictly follow the existing rules and regulations.

We recommended that Management should look into this matter seriously and provide measures that would address the underperformance of the personnel concerned. Close supervision is highly recommended including monitoring of their accomplishments. Instruct the Accountant to monitor the submission of the Barangay transaction documents and send letters regularly to those who failed to submit in order to hasten submission, thereby promoting prompt preparation and submission of financial reports.
The Accountant committed to adhere to the audit recommendation.

We recommended that Management stop allowing reimbursement of payment of fuel and oil. Ensure that all money payments by the LGU to suppliers are deducted with the prescribed withholding tax pursuant to the existing laws and revenue regulations of the BIR. Any penalties impose by the BIR as a result of non-withholding of taxes shall be shouldered by the officials concerned.
The audit recommendation was well taken by the Management.

We recommended that the Management submit supporting documents to prove that the subject buildings or facilities are government properties to substantiate the electricity expense amounting to ₱425,117.75, otherwise the properties will be deemed privately-owned and the related expense will be considered illegal expenditure. Henceforth, ensure that payment of electricity expense shall be made only to government/LGU properties.
8.8 The GSO assured that this matter will be further investigated and the results thereof will be submitted to the Audit Team.

We recommended that Management ensure that the projects to be funded under the 20% DF are properly planned and implementation ready as prescribed under DBM-DOF-DILG Joint Memorandum No. 1. See to it that the 20% of the Internal Revenue Allotment will be transferred monthly to the 20% DF account to assure sufficient fund to implement the projects.
9.13 The MPDC committed to adhere to the audit recommendation and the Accountant assured that the 20% of the IRA will be transferred to its appropriate account monthly to ensure availability of funds for the projects.

We recommended that Management ensure that the plans and programs are prepared in such a way that it can be readily implemented in the implementation year to avoid unnecessary revision which would delay the completion of the project. Any deviation/changes should be supported with approved Variation Order and furnish the Budget and Accounting Office for their reference for proper monitoring of charges and disbursements covered in an appropriation for labor, materials, etc. and any noted deficiencies will be immediately addressed.
The Management agreed to the audit recommendation.

We recommended that Management seek the authority from the Sangguniang Bayan before any charges are made to the lump-sum appropriation. Remind the Local Finance Committee to be diligent in the review of PPAs and to discourage approval of PPAs which are in generic terms, otherwise, secure proper authority from the sanggunian concerned.
11.8   The officers concerned assured to comply with the requirements relative to lumpsum appropriation and committed to review properly the documentation of the projects before inclusion in the Annual Investment Plan.

We recommended that Management come up with possible measures to avoid further incurrence of cash overdraft. Ensure that the 20% of IRA be transferred to its appropriate bank account every month to guaranty available funds for the identified development projects.   See to it that any savings from the LGU’s operations at any given year will be applied first to the cash deficit in 20% DF and utilize the fund exclusively for its intended purpose.
12.8 Management agreed to adhere to the audit recommendation.

We recommended that the Management discontinue the practice of inappropriate use of the funds to ensure that the programmed DRRM PPAs are backed-up with sufficient cash, thereby ensuring its full implementation during the budget year and to guaranty transfer of unutilized DRRMF to Special Trust as prescribed in Section 5.1.10 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
13.15  The Management is well-aware of the current financial condition of the LGU and committed to adhere to the audit recommendation.

14.5 We recommended that Management deduct the contract deficiency amounting to ₱1,202,912.34 from the remaining claims of the contractor.
14.6 The officer concerned stated that they had already submitted their justification, but failed to provide proof of submission.
14.7 The deficiency is sustained until submission of valid explanation on the matter.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. create a dedicated Inventory Committee that could focus on conducting a
thorough, complete, and independent physical count of all Municipal real
properties and semi-expendable items, preparation of property cards and one-
time cleansing of PPE as required under COA Circular No. 2020-006. Require
periodic inventory report to monitor the status of their count and the
submission of the annual inventory report not later than January 31 of the
following year for approval, a copy furnished to the COA Auditor; and
b. direct the Inventory Committee to maintain an updated PPE property cards,
and reconcile results of inventory with the accounting records to arrive at the
proper balance of the PPE accounts. Any unserviceable properties should be
counted separately and for disposal periodic reconciliation of these records
with subsidy ledgers of the Municipal Accountant.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer effect the
cancellation of the stale checks and furnish the Municipal Accountant with the
original disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents for the preparation of
Journal Entry Voucher in the municipality’s books of accounts to fairly present its
financial statements as of December 31, 2021.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
reconcile with the Municipal Treasurer the P350.00 unreconciled balance between the
General Ledger and the Cashbook. Prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reflect
the correct balance of the Cash in Local Treasury account in the financial statements
as of December 31, 2021.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Budget Officer to present accurately the reconciliation of the Statement of
Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts with the Statement of Financial
Performance pursuant to Sections 15 and 21 of Philippine Public Sector Accounting
Standards I  Presentation of Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the planning team of the LGU to:
a. conduct proper planning of Covid-19 programs and activities to quickly
respond to the risks of Covid-19 to the constituents of the Municipality; and
b. ensure that all Covid-19 PPAs that are to be implemented by the Municipality
should be clearly specified as to amount, name, nature, and place and be
included in the AIP and budget.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Development
Council to prepare a Supplemental or Amended AIP for newly identified PPAs and
cause the approval by the Sanggunian.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
coordinate with the responsible officials to facilitate the immediate submission of the
lacking documents. Henceforth, ensure that all documentary requirements for
emergency purchase are complied with before certifying as to completeness of the
supporting documents of the disbursement vouchers.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Planning and
Development Coordinator (MPDC) and the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that
changes in the annual budget be made in accordance with Article 417 and 454 (b) of
the Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA No. 7160. Likewise, ensure that
utilization of 20 per cent Development Fund is all embodied in the Annual Investment
Plan/Program.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor establish the Municipal Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Office composing of the designated Municipal DRRM
Officer and at least staffs for the effective and efficient disaster risk reduction
planning and management.
We also recommended the designated MDRRMO, with the assistance of authorized
staffs to:
a. carefully plan specific disaster preparedness and mitigation programs and
activities and ensure implementation thereof in order to maximize the LDRRM
Fund; and
b. update annually the list of projects and activities to be funded from LDRRMF
specially on the unexpended QRF and DRRM  MOOE of the previous years
transferred to Special Trust Fund.

With these, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Bids and Awards
Committee (BAC) and Technical Working Group (TWG):
a) to scrutinize the sufficiency of the winning bidders manpower on all the
projects to be awarded and not on an individual basis only in cases where the
latter is the lowest bidder in several projects which have similar or overlapping
implementation period in order to oblige the winning contractor to supplement
its manpower to be utilized in the projects awarded to it or to disqualify it from
obtaining several awards or contracts in conformity with the provisions of
Section 34.1 and 34.3 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184;
b) to monitor contractors with on-going projects with other government agencies
and to coordinate with them involved in implementing infrastructure projects
not disclosed in the Statement of On-going and Completed projects to avoid
overlapping and delay in project accomplishment and/or substandard quality of
work;
c) to refrain from entering into contract with the contractors if it is after 10 days
from the receipt of the notice of award, cancel the award and forfeit the bid
security of the said bidder and award the project to the contractor next to
lowest calculated bid. Likewise, ensure that after the award of projects, the
said project to be documented with a contract and issued with the Notice to
Proceed (NTP) for the project should be started immediately for
implementation by the contractor within the 20 day period so that delay will
not be incurred in project implementation due to late issuance of NTP; and
d) to ensure that pertinent provisions of IRR of RA No. 9184 are strictly adhered
to promote transparency and economy of its procurement process.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer to
strictly and closely monitor all projects simultaneously implemented by the
contractors and prepare an individual status report per project to be submitted to the
Honorable Mayor for monitoring purposes and in order to check, verify and evaluate
the quality of work and efficiency of the said contractors.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
immediately submit the original disbursement vouchers and complete supporting
documents to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the honorable members of the
Sanggunian include among its top priorities the construction of a building for the
safekeeping and accommodation of the increasing volume of documents generated
by the various departments/offices of the Municipality, particularly those for
submissions to the audit team inconsideration of the preservation period of ten years
as mandated by law.

Also, the Local Finance Committee and other officials concerned ensure that the funds will be applied to its intended purpose for the full attainment of the objective for which the fund was established.

observations.recommendation:
1.11. We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to direct the
Local IATF to continuously enforce the existing health protocols being implemented
in the community and ensure that adequate health consciousness is instilled among
its people.

2.11. We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant
to (a) immediately record all valid reconciling items that require adjustments; (b)
explore other options to clear the long outstanding reconciling items when all efforts
to locate the supporting documents has been exhausted; and (c) Municipal Treasurer
to (i) send written notice to the payees at least one month before the checks become
stale; and (ii) request for additional staff to augment the workforce in their office.

3.14. Pending actual implementations, we reiterated our recommendation that the
Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to (a) immediately issue the
final demand letters to the persons responsible for outstanding cash advances; (b)
stop granting cash advances to employees with unliquidated balances; (c) cause the
withholding of their salaries in case of unjustified failure to liquidate their cash
advances; (d) file appropriate administrative or criminal charges, as warranted; (e)
ensure that all cash advances granted to elective officials are for travel purposes only;
and (f) strengthen its internal control system on the grant and liquidation of cash
advances to avoid the recurrence.

4.7. We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the (a)
Municipal Accountant to (i) prioritize the retrieval of the supporting documents to
further evaluate and analyze the accounts with abnormal balances so that necessary
adjusting entries can be made; (ii) prepare the adjusting entries for the completed
projects that were already liquidated to the Source and Agency; and (iii) ensure that
the journal entries would not result in negative balances of subsidiary accounts; (b)
Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the return of all unexpended balance of fund
transfer to the source agency after the completion of the projects for maximum
utilization of funds; and (c) concerned officials to adhere with the provisions of the
guidelines on the release and utilization of funds from National Government Agencies
for the benefit of the general public.

5.6. We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) create an
Inventory Committee who shall (i) in coordination with the Property Division/Unit,
plan/strategize on how to conduct and complete the physical inventory within the
prescribed period by preparing a Physical Inventory Plan; and (ii) be temporarily
relieved of all their regular duties to devote their full time in the conduct of the
physical inventory taking until the same is completed; (b) consider the option for a
“One Time Cleansing of PPE accounts” in accordance with the guidelines of COA
Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020; and (c) instruct the Municipal
Assessor and Acting Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the titling of the 24 parcels of
land in the Municipality name.
5.7. We also reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the LCE consider
creating a position for, or designating a property officer who will take charge of the
management and custodial functions of the properties of the Municipality as it
promotes proper segregation of functions.

7.7. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) capacitate the Municipal
Disaster Risk Reduction Office by providing them the necessary seminar/training
pertinent to their function; (b) prioritize the procurement of disaster related
equipment; (c) fill up the position for DRRM Assistant when legal prohibitions are
lifted; and (d) implement and monitor the proper use of the rescue boat, or all
government property for that matter.

8.16. We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) incorporate in its GPB a more
creative means of reaching out to a larger scale of beneficiaries while observing health
protocols; (b) refrain from granting additional incentives for GAD service providers
with no legal basis; and (c) seek acknowledgment from DILG on any changes or re-
appropriation of future GPB.

9.4. We commended the efforts of Management and recommended, on which the
LCE agreed, to instruct the Accounting Office to continuously comply with the
existing tax laws and regulations particularly on the withholding of taxes and
remittance thereof within the prescribed period.

10.8. We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) appoint an Agency Authorized
Officer (AAO) who shall implement the measures required by the GSIS; and (b)
instruct the Municipal Accounting Staff to (i) continuously coordinate with the GSIS
for the reconciliation of remittances while the AAO was not yet appointed; (ii)
facilitate enrolment of the BP numbers of concerned employees; (iii) continue
updating the salary information of all of its employees; and (iv) regularly reconcile
the remittances moving forward.

11.3. We commended the efforts of Management and recommended, on which the
LCE agreed, to instruct the Accounting Office to continuously comply with the
existing rules and regulations on the withholding of PhilHealth contributions and loan
amortization due from employees and remittance thereof within the prescribed
period.

12.5. We commended the efforts of Management and recommended, on which the
LCE agreed, to instruct the Accounting Office to continuously comply with the
existing rules and regulations on the withholding of HDMF contributions and loan
amortization due from employees and remittance thereof within the prescribed
period.

13.1 However, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the
concerned departments/offices to (a) submit an explanation on the noted observations
on specific transactions discussed previously and submit all the lacking documents;
and (b) religiously abide (i) by the rules and regulations on procurement set forth in
RA 9184; and (ii) by the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14,
2012 on the documentary requirements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to disclose the required reconciliation either on the face of the SCBAA or in the Notes to Financial Statements in compliance with paragraph 47 of PPSAS 24 to achieve disclosure on transparency in financial reporting.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant had submitted balances on reconciliation but full disclosure on the reconciliation of surplus with the net cash flows from operating activities was not provided. Reiterated with modifications under Observation No. 4, Part II, Page No. 38 of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant effect the necessary adjusting entries for the recording of the accounts payable for the balance of contract cost to the contractor as a result of the final billing and 100 per cent completion of the project and transfer the costs of the completed projects from Construction in Progress - Infrastructure account to Infrastructure Assets account.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant provided adjusting entries for this.
observations.reason: Depreciation expenses for this were provided and proper Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) was made; Implementation provided.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and also requested the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office to submit an explanation letter for the 199 ineligible beneficiaries that were given subsidies, to submit to the Audit Team the photos of the beneficiaries during the actual receipt of SAP, the original Memorandum of Agreement and the original copies of payroll on Social Amelioration for further evaluation of the fund utilization.
observations.managementAction: The MSWDO had provided justification and submitted soft copies of the photos of the beneficiaries during the actual receipt of SAP. The Municipal MSWDO provided the required explanation letter for some of the beneficiaries.
observations.reason: Other photos of the beneficiaries during the actual receipt of SAP of 18 Barangays were corrupted in the computer. Some supporting documents were not fully submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. require the involved offices to immediately submit the lacking documents to validate propriety and reasonableness of the procurement made by the Municipality; and
b. require concerned officials to strictly comply with the provisions of GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 and IRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: The MPDC and Bids and Awards Committee had submitted some of the lacking disbursement documents. The Municipal Accountant stated that she is coordinating with the responsible departments for the submission of required documents and fulfilment of the audit recommendations.
observations.reason: Various discrepancies contrary to IRR of RA No. 9184 were noted. Some missing documents were submitted but others remained lacking.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to create a special account in the general fund for the Bayanihan Grant to Cities and Municipalities for monitoring/tracking of the utilization of the allocation, pursuant to Section 3.7 of Local Budget Circular No. 125.
observations.managementAction: The MPDC/BAC Secretary submitted some documentation and the Municipal Office created tracking of expenditures for the BGCM.
observations.reason: This was implemented for the succeeding Bayanihan Grants.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) advise the Municipal Project Management and Implementation Unit (MPMIU) to closely monitor the projects that will be identified and programmed under the PRDP, if any, in their future transactions. We also recommended that the LCE advise the MPMIU to exert more effort in resolving right of way issues and documentary requirements to avoid delays for their future project implementation.
observations.managementAction: This is for the succeeding projects on PRDP. The MPMIU monitored and coordinated to resolve right of way issues and complied with PRDP documentary requirements.
observations.reason: Implementation for the succeeding PRDP projects was undertaken; compliance achieved.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
• Provide adjusting journal entry for the errors committed in recording of the depreciation expenses for various PPE accounts to arrive at accurate amount of PPE.
• Reconcile the unreconciled depreciation expenses recorded in the financial statements versus depreciation expenses in the lapsing schedules by preparing the all lapsing schedule with complete information consistent with the balances presented in the financial statements with emphasis on providing details on those PPEs which are described as “Beginning Balance, Balance Forwarded, Labor and Materials, General Journal”. Always use 10 per cent salvage value in the computation of the depreciation expenses; and
• Provide depreciation expenses for prior year periods especially for Infrastructure Accounts. Depreciation expenses for prior period should be charged to Prior Year’s Adjustment.
observations.managementAction: No action made. The Accounting Office had not provided the detailed Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) and further reconciliation.
observations.reason: No action was provided to the Audit Team by the Accounting Office; depreciation entries for prior periods were not fully provided.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the following:
• Municipal Accountant to take up the necessary adjusting entries in the books for the collection of delinquent RPT and SET Receivables.
• Municipal Accountant to maintain the necessary subsidiary ledgers for the RPT and SET receivables to validate balances.
• Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit the Certified List of Delinquent Taxpayers as of year-end in time for the closing of the books and to the Sanggunian Bayan for assistance in collection; and
• Coordinate with the Sangguniang Bayan to adopt measures to collect from delinquent taxpayers and strengthen collection system.
observations.managementAction: No action made. The Municipal Treasurer did not submit the list of RPT and SET Receivables to the Accounting Office. Municipal Accounting Office staff had health issues; reconciliation efforts limited.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant had not fully reconciled and provide the required subsidiary ledgers; the Municipal Treasurer did not submit certified list of delinquent taxpayers; not prioritized due to staffing and health issues.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to present accurately the reconciliation of the budget actual amounts with the statement of financial performance pursuant to Sections 15 and 21 of Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards I – Presentation of Financial Statements.
observations.reason: There were discrepancies and inaccurate presentations noted; reconciliation for CY 2021 still required adjustment. Reiterated with modifications under Observation No. 4, Part II, Page No. 38 of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to evaluate the validity of long outstanding payables and seek authority, if needed from the Sanggunian Bayan for the reversion of the long outstanding payables to Unappropriated Surplus to reflect the correct balance of payables as of year-end, in pursuant to RA No. 3526 as implemented by EO No. 109, s.1993.
observations.reason: Some adjustment actions were taken as of December 31, 2021 but full reversion/adjustment not completed; outstanding payables remained as of year-end.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• Require the Accounting Office to analyze the accounts with old balances that remained unreconciled/unadjusted over the years and prepare necessary adjusting entries to correct the account balance, if any; and
• Require the responsible officials/employees to remit immediately in full the withheld funds to Bureau of Internal Revenue and Pag-IBIG, PhilHealth and GSIS.
observations.managementAction: No action made. Some remittances were processed but reconciliation with accounts was not completed.
observations.reason: Some remittances for December 2021 were made but the accounts were not fully reconciled as of year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct concerned officials and employees to require the submission of duly filled-up Driver’s Trip Tickets to support the payment of gasoline, oil and lubricants; ensure that driver’s trip tickets are serially numbered and properly filled up, and to prepare and submit monthly reports of fuel consumption and official travels summarizing trips.
observations.managementAction: The GSO had submitted the Monthly report of Fuel Consumption of Motor Vehicles of the Municipality. Some drivers are practicing filling-up of driver’s trip tickets. However actual distance travelled could not be completed for some service vehicles due to defective odometers.
observations.reason: Some trip tickets cannot be fully filled up due to defective odometers of some service vehicles; some monthly reports submitted but not complete.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Bayan Secretary to prepare a complete implementing guidelines on the Scholarship Program to have the complete basis for the effective and efficient delivery of service to its constituents. The guidelines can include creation of Scholarship Board Committee, step by step screening process, income level requirements, courses allowed, service obligation and consequence on non-compliance. Submit the approved implementing guidelines to the Sanggunian Bayan for the passage of an ordinance establishing a Municipal Scholarship Program.
observations.managementAction: The MENRO and concerned offices prepared drafts and some implementing procedures. However, final implementing guidelines were not submitted or approved by the Sanggunian.
observations.reason: No final implementing guidelines were prepared and submitted; not prioritized by the concerned office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the GAD Council:
• Submit to the Audit Team the GAD Accomplishment Report and follow required formats and include consistent data for accurate assessment; 
• Perform gender analysis and utilize the GAD Fund only for programs, projects and activities that address identified gender issues; and
• Prepare consolidated GAD Accomplishment Report based from the DILG-approved GPB following prescribed instructions and format.
observations.managementAction: Some GAD activities were implemented and the Municipal MSWDO submitted partial accomplishment reports. Required formats were not fully followed in preparation of GAD Plan and Budget for 2019.
observations.reason: Required format in preparation of the GAD Plan and Budget was not followed; some data corrupted; consolidated GAD AR not submitted in required format.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MENRO in coordination with the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to facilitate the review and update of the Ten Year Solid Waste Management Plan which shall be consistent with the National SWM Framework and to submit for approval by the government agencies concerned as mandated by law.
observations.managementAction: As per Municipal Agriculturist, they will include in the budget the cost for consultant who will guide in the finalization and completion of the revised Solid Waste Management Plan. The MPDC will include in the budget the cost of consultant.
observations.reason: Draft revisions prepared; consultant costs to be budgeted; plan still pending finalization and approval.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• Implement the Municipal Ordinance No. 08-2015 "No Segregation, No Collection Policy" among residents of every barangay; and
• Exercise political will to implement environmental guidelines in preserving and protecting the environment as mandated by RA 9003.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Officials and the MENRO enjoined barangay officials and residents to implement the segregation and no collection policy. Some barangays and citizens complied while others were non-compliant.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

(a) maintain and keep updated the subsidiary ledgers for PPE; and 

(b) coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Engineer for the proper recognition of infrastructure assets in the books.

We also recommended that the Property/Supply Officer maintain for each class of PPE a Property Card (PC) for the recording of PPE with information as to its description, custody and disposal.

Further, we reiterated our recommendation that the LCE create an Inventory and Appraisal Committee that will be tasked to conduct a thorough review and physical count of the property and equipment of the Municipality in order to present a realistic balance of property accounts and make a fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Development Council, chaired by the Local Chief Executive, strengthen the cycle of planning, execution, monitoring and evaluation of programs and projects for prioritization under the 20% DF in order to ensure well prepared, and implementation-ready programs and projects to continuously provide new and improved services to the Municipal’s constituents and the public.

We commended the Management for its proper accounting of receipt and distribution of donated relief goods and those purchased through the utilization of cash donations as required by COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014. 

Nonetheless, henceforth, we recommended that the Management strictly follow the other provisions of the same Circular, specifically the:

(a) proper recording of cash donations in the Trust Liabilities – DRRMF under the Trust Fund books; and 

(b) all other reportorial requirements for cash and in-kind donations stated therein.

Also, we recommended that the Management:

(a) submit the latest Report on the Receipt and Distribution/Issuance of Donated Relief Goods to the COA Office; and 

(b) coordinate with the Provincial Government of Pampanga (PGP) for the immediate identification of the costing of relief goods received therefrom which shall serve as the basis of recording of remaining relief items/goods in the books and registries as required by COA Circular 2020-009 dated April 21, 2020.
However, we recommended that Management coordinate with the Provincial Government of Pampanga to facilitate the proper accounting of the ₱100,000.00 cash incentive.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC):

(a) submit the lacking documents; and 

(b) strictly observe the provisions of the revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 particularly the (i) posting of NOA, Contract/PO and NTP; (ii) preparation and submission of the PERT/CPM network; (iii) submission of DOLE Approved Construction Safety and Health Program of bidders/contractors; and (iv) required procedures and documents for procurements through negotiated procurement under Two-Failed Biddings.

Further, we recommended that the Municipal Engineer prepare and issue the necessary documents in case of temporary work suspension.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct all municipal offices to ensure that coordination between implementing departments be done, for future programs of the same nature, to avoid incurrence of the same.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the HRMO, in coordination with the Department Heads, to submit the (a) approved schedule of employees who were required to work during the ECQ; and (b) Accomplishment Reports of the Job Order personnel.

We commended the Municipality’s considerable efforts for their compliance with the mechanisms and processes in accordance with the provisions of PCW-NEDA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-01 and PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 or Guidelines on the Localization of the Magna Carta of Women.

However, we recommended that the Management:

(a) continuously exert efforts for the updating and completion of the sex-disaggregated GAD database; and 

(b) perform the necessary courses of actions to (i) update the GAD FPS and GAD Code; (ii) designate specific GAD TWG Members and Secretariat; and (iii) establish the GAD Monitoring and Evaluation (M&E) Team, in accordance with the guidelines.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Accounting Department:

(a) ensure the proper and timely remittance of withheld taxes to avoid penalty; and 

(b) conduct verification of unreconciled balances.

We reiterated our prior years’ recommendation and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to seek the assistance of the depository banks to recapture or reconstitute prior years’ bank transactions as a take-off action in the identification and documentation of the reconciling items that had been left unrecorded in the books for so long.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct Municipal Engineer to

Conduct extensive planning in the preparation of program of works (POWs) of succeeding projects to avoid similar problems that cause delay in their implementation to the detriment of the intended beneficiaries and the general public; and

We recommended and management agreed that the

Staff at the Municipal Treasurer’s Office strictly observe the proper sub-coding of income accounts particularly for economic enterprise in the preparation of RCD; and open a separate bank account for the economic enterprise and

The staff of the Accounting Office
Cause the accurate recording and summarizing of income and expenses data for the economic enterprise;

Prepare the corresponding financial statements to form part of the schedules of General Fund trial balance to serve as a tool in informing top management on whether or not the income generated by the economic enterprise/s are sufficient to meet their operating cost and enable management to evaluate them for the purpose of recommending corrective measures for the improvement of the economic enterprise/s.

order to fully attain the purpose and intent for establishing the fund at the soonest
possible time, thereby, maximizing the real value of the money on hand.

review and update of the Ten Year Solid Waste Management Plan which shall be
consistent with the National SWM Framework and with its current situation and
accomplishments, for review and approval by the government agencies concerned as
mandated by law. Such 10 year plan must hold an approval from the National SWM

implement the Municipal Ordinance 08-2015 or the “No Segregation, No Collection
Policy” among residents of every barangay.
We also recommended the Municipal Officials and Employees to exercise
strong political will to implement the environmental laws on preserving and
protecting the environment as mandated by the Act.

We recommended that the:
a. MDRRMO carefully plan disaster preparedness and mitigation programs
and activities with emphasis on assessment of the conditions of
barangays and other parts of the territorial area of the Municipality to
identify high disaster risk areas, identification of specific and actual
projects needed with emphasis on the total budget available. Ensure
implementation thereof in order to maximize the LDRRM Fund. Strengthen
the rescue and recovery facilities, manpower and equipment to ensure
readiness in case of urgency; and
b. MDRRMO update annually the list of projects and activities to be funded
from LDRRMF specially on the unexpended QRF and DRRM 	6 MOOE of the
previous years transferred to Special Trust Fund.

the DRRMF-MOOE to Special Trust Fund in the Trust Fund books everyyear-end and

and regulations on grant of cash advances be enforced strictly and to
issue demand letters to officials and employees with outstanding cash

provisions of R.A. 9184 and it’s IRR, BIR Regulations on withholding
of taxes, and COA Circular No. 97-002 to preclude same
unfavourable observation in the future and suspension in audit of

Accountant to reconcile the reciprocal accounts and effect

amount of PRDP funds transferred to the General Fund. Trust funds
shall only be used for specific purpose for which it was created
pursuant to Section 309 (b) of the Local Government Code. Refrain

pertinent rules and regulations in the grant of honorarium to

and operations of the local government unit with emphasis on Section

disallowance.
