observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, as Chairperson of the LCPC, direct the members of the Council to prepare the Local Development Plan for Children and Local Investment Plan for Children and allocate one percent (1%) of IRA for the strengthening and implementation of programs of the LCPC pursuant to Section 15 of R.A. 9344.
observations.managementAction: The LCPC was established by the Municipality in CY 2019. The 1% allocation of IRA was provided to the LCPC in CY 2019.
observations.reason: The matter was not prioritized by the concerned officials.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality duly consider the instructive guidelines on the creation or establishment of the local economic enterprise provided in the Manual on Setting Up and Operation of Local Economic Enterprises for Local Government Units and prepare the required feasibility study and business plan prior to declaring enterprises.
observations.managementAction: Starting CY 2019, the Lying-In Center was no longer operated as a local economic enterprise, since it was not self-sustaining and was heavily subsidized by municipal funds.
observations.reason: The matter was not prioritized by the concerned units; the enterprise was not self-sustaining and was subsidized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality include among its top priorities the construction of a building for the safekeeping and accommodation of the increasing volume of documents generated by the various departments/offices of the Municipality, particularly those for submission to the Audit Team, with consideration on the preservation period of ten (10) years as mandated by law.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by the Municipality due to lack of available space and budget.
observations.reason: Lack of available space and budget.

observations.recommendation:
1.10 Considering that this is a reiteration of the previous years’ audit observation,
we recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive instruct the
(a) Municipal Accountant to (i) initiate the conduct of reconciliation of the Cash-in-
Bank records with the Treasurer’s cash records at least on a quarterly basis; and (ii)
determine the appropriate action or disposition of the reconciling items that are
without available supporting documents; and
(b) the Municipal Treasurer and the Accountant to seek the assistance of the
depository banks to recapture the necessary information to support the adjustments
for unresolved items in the bank reconciliation statements.

2.14 We reiterated our previous year's recommendation and management agreed
that the Municipal Accountant (a) send final demand letters to the concerned
accountable officers for the immediate liquidation of their accountabilities and cause
the withholding of their salaries in case of unjustified failure to do so; (b) refrain from
granting additional cash advances to accountable officers with unsettled previous cash
advances; (c) file appropriate legal action, if warranted, against those who have failed
to liquidate the overdue cash advances after exhausting appropriate means, including
those who are no longer in the government service; and (d) ensure that all cash
advances granted to an elective official are for travel purposes only as prescribed
under COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.

1.13. We reiterated our recommendations that the (a) Local Chief Executive create
a committee composed of personnel with the necessary competencies and
determination to conduct the physical count of inventories and perform
reconciliation efforts for the timely submission of the required report thereon
pursuant to the Manual on the NGAS; and (b) Accounting Office strictly comply
with the policies on the proper reporting and recording of inventories pursuant to
the same Manual.
1.14. Further, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive require all
departments to maintain the inventory records as prescribed by the Manual on the
NGAS.
1.15. Moreover, we recommended that the Accounting Office (i) coordinate with
the Municipal Treasurer, other concerned departments, and end-users on the
reconciliation of the Inventory account balances; (ii) conduct thorough
analysis/review of the inventory accounts; and (iii) make the necessary adjusting
entries to reflect the correct balances of the accounts.

2.3. We reiterated our recommendation and Management agreed that the Local
Chief Executive (a) create an Inventory and Appraisal Committee who will be
tasked to conduct the physical inventory of the Municipality’s properties, perform
reconciliation efforts for the timely submission of the required report thereon
pursuant to the Manual on the NGAS; (b) require the maintenance and keeping of
the required property records; and (c) instruct the Municipal Assessor to secure
documents on the transfer/donation of parcels of land made to GPSAT to be used as
basis in the de-recognition of the same in the books of accounts.
2.4. Further, we recommended that Rural Health Units’ Heads furnish the
Accounting Department with documents evidencing the transfer of properties from
the Department of Health and other donors.

3.7. For the foregoing observations, we recommended and management agreed to
refer to COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 for the documentary
requirements for infrastructure projects.

4.8. We reiterated our recommendation and Management agreed to adopt
control measures on the grant of financial assistance to its component barangays
and ensure that the disbursements are supported with SB Resolution authorizing
such transactions.

5.52. We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive (a)
submit an explanation/justification on the noted observations/deviations on implemented projects;
(b) ensure that projects programmed for implementation are capital
investments for implementation during the budget year rather than on
“as needed basis” and also ensure that PPAs are aligned with the
Philippine Development Plan, the Medium-Term Public Investment
Program;
(c) require the Municipal Planning and Development Council and
Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that the specific projects proposed
for local funding are included in the Annual Budget in accordance with
applicable laws;
(d) require the Municipal Engineer to (i) refer to DPWH DO. No. 52, series
of 2012 for the necessary documents for civil works in case of temporary
work suspension and Annex E of RA No. 9184 for the guidelines in the
preparation and submission of Variation/Change/Extra Work Order;
and (ii) check the progress of the construction at all reasonable times
during the project implementation to ensure that there is no negative
slippage; and
(e) require the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant to ensure
that documents are complete before payment to contractor is made.

6.24. However, we recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief
Executive instruct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management
Officer/Council to ensure the timely submission of the Report on Sources and
Utilization of DRRMF in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-
002.

7.29. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, through the Municipal
Solid Waste Management Board (a) if possible, provide additional manpower to the
MENRO to ensure the regular monitoring of the implementation of SWM-related
projects and ordinances and to aid in the strict imposition of fines and penalties
against violators of prohibited acts; and (b) continue to educate the public through
the intensive conduct of continuous forums and seminars to raise awareness and
increase the compliance rate of households and residents.

8.6. For the foregoing observation, we recommended that the Local Chief
Executive, through the Municipal Engineer, require the Contractor to cause the
repair of the portion of the project found defective at no additional cost to the
Municipality.

9.12. In view of the noted observations, we recommended that management:
(a) institute segregation of incompatible duties and responsibilities to
prevent errors and fraud by ensuring that no single person has
complete control over the assessment, collection and recording
function;
(b) instruct the designated staff in the releasing and recording of business
permits to (i) use the original OR as basis in the recording of paid fees
and charges, (ii) keep true copies of the original OR and issued
Mayor’s Business Permits on file, and (iii) ensure that postings made
in the logbook and permits are correct and accurate;
(c) direct the designated BPLO/collecting officer to restitute the
unreported collections amounting to ₱55,750.48;
(d) cause the immediate relief of the erring officer from duties and
responsibilities as collecting officer and transfer her to a position
where monetary accountability is not involved; and,
(e) ensure that internal control policies on the assessment, receipt and
collection of taxes, charges and fees for the application/renewal of
business permits are strictly observed and implemented.

10.13. We recommended that the Head of the Human Resource Management Office
(a) conduct periodic performance evaluation and assessment of Job Order personnel
to ensure value for money and avoid wastage of government funds; and (b) refer to
the provisions of CSC Resolution No. 02-0790 dated June 5, 2002 and CSC
Memorandum Circular No. 24, series of 2002 for the policy guidelines for contract
of services and job order.

11.9. We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive
require the concerned Municipal Officials and employees to submit an explanation
on the noted audit observations as basis for the next audit action.

11.10. Henceforth, we recommended that the Municipal Drivers prepare Monthly
Report of Fuel Consumption and Monthly Report of Official Travels showing the
type of vehicle, total distance traveled, total fuel used, and the total amount of fuel
purchased pursuant to the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government
Motor Vehicles.

12.9. We recommended and Management agreed to (a) submit the required
documents to substantiate the regularity of the transaction; (b) strictly follow the
guidelines and methodologies for the acquisition of private properties for
government development projects; and (c) ensure the completeness of the
documentary requirements as enumerated in COA Circular No. 2012-001.

13.10. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the GFPS to (a)
exert efforts to come up with a comprehensive GAD database/sex-disaggregated
data to serve as a reliable basis for planning, budgeting, programming and policy
formulation; (b) guarantee the efficient use of the GAD budget thru the
identification and effective implementation of GAD-related PPAs; (c) strictly
comply with the provisions of the Magna Carta of Women and PCW-DILG-DBM-
NEDA Joint Circular No. 2012-01.

14.6. We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to ensure the
proper and timely remittance of withheld taxes.

15.5. We reiterated our recommendation and the Municipal Accountant agreed to
continue with the efforts of coordinating with the GSIS regarding reconciliation of
records/accounts.

15.6. Further, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to address
the issue on discrepancies noted on the withheld contributions and loan
amortization and their remittances to avoid the imposition of undue penalties to the
members.

16.6. For the deficiencies noted, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant
agreed to conduct a thorough analysis of the account to validate the over remittance
and discrepancies on the amounts withheld and remitted.

17.10. We reiterated our recommendation and the Municipal Accountant agreed to
conduct a thorough analysis of the Due to PhilHealth account to determine the
composition of its balance and make the necessary adjustments in the books to
reflect the correct balance.

18.4. We recommended and Management agreed to strictly enforce the settlement
of the audit disallowances and charges within the prescribed period, pursuant to the
2009 Revised Rules and Regulations on the Settlement of Accounts of the
Commission on Audit.

• Direct the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete inventory of all the
Municipality’s properties and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of
Property, Plant and Equipment to be submitted to the Auditor concerned not
later than January 31 of each year;
• Require the Municipal Accountant and GSO/Property Officer to maintain
complete and updated PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively,
immediately reconcile the results of inventory per PPE account and accordingly
effect adjustments.
• Instruct the Municipal Accountant to review the balances of the reported
Property, Plant and Equipment in view of the discrepancy of ₱13,631,703.50
between the reported purchase and construction of PPE in the Cash Flow
Statement and the total additions to PPE in the Notes to Financial Statements in
CY 2018; and,
• Immediately comply with the accounting and reporting guidelines provided in
COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015 to fairly present the LGU’s
financial statements.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor:
 Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit to the Municipal
Accounting Office the Certified List of Delinquent Taxpayers as of year-end in
time for the closing of the books. Likewise, submit to the Sangguniang Bayan
the certified list of all real property tax delinquencies which remained
uncollected or unpaid for at least one (1) year and a statement of the reason or
reasons for non-collection or non-payment on or before December 31 of the year
immediately succeeding the year in which the delinquencies were incurred, with
a request for assistance in the enforcement of the remedies for collection;
 Direct the Municipal Accountant to conduct a detailed review of the RPT and
SET Receivables accounts and investigate the cause of the differences between
the accounts. Thereafter, require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal
Treasurer to reconcile their records and adjust accordingly based on
determined disparities between the set-up, payments, and any other
adjustments made in both records;
 Instruct the Municipal Accountant to take up the necessary adjusting entries in
the books for the collection of delinquent RPT and SET Receivables.
Subsequently, follow the prescribed accounting entries for the set-up of RPT
and SET receivables, receipt of payment and distribution of RPT and SET
collections; and,
 Coordinate with the Sangguniang Bayan Members and adopt measures to
collect from delinquent taxpayers, and strengthen and intensify the collection
system and tax campaign.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require the:
• Municipal Treasurer to a) prepare and submit to the Municipal Accounting Office
the List of Unreleased Checks in time for the closing of the books to enable the
reversion back to cash in bank and payable accounts of the amount of
unreleased checks in order to fairly present the cash in bank and other affected
accounts in the financial statements; and b) for the checks which have become
stale, send written notice to the payees for the existence of checks and require
submission to facilitate issuance of new checks;
• Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team the Journal Entry Vouchers
(JEVs) for the adjustments of the deficiencies noted herein and effect the
necessary adjustments of reconciling items determined during the preparation
of the Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements on a timely basis for an accurate
and reliable Cash in Bank account balance in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
observe the disclosure requirements in the preparation of the financial statements in
accordance with the PPSASs and Section 3.4 of COA Circular No. 2016-004 to assists
users in understanding the financial statements and to provide them with information
relevant to decision-making.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant properly withhold creditable income
taxes of 5% or 10%, whichever is applicable, from the honoraria paid to employees of
national government agencies and remit the same to the BIR through BIR Form No.
0605 on a monthly basis and BIR Form No. 1601-EQ on a quarterly basis, to help the
government accelerate the collection of income.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant subject the honoraria paid to
employees of the municipal government to withholding tax on compensation, in
accordance with the prescribed rates under RMC No. 105-2017, and remit the same to
the BIR through the monthly BIR Form No. 1601-C.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• Enjoin the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to (a) reconcile
employees’ records at GSIS, PhilHealth and Pag-IBIG to identify the affected
employees on the unremitted balances and immediately remit the amounts due
to these agencies; and, (b) ensure that mandatory contributions are remitted
within the prescribed period.
• Require the Municipal Accountant to (a) identify the cause/s of under and over
remittance for some accounts; (b) reconcile the amount of contributions
actually remitted with the year-end balances of Due to GSIS, Due to Pag-IBIG
and Due to PhilHealth reported in the financial statements, and adjust the
records accordingly.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
 Direct the General Services Officer to refrain from purchasing common-use
supplies through shopping, unless the procurement is emergency in nature or
the items being purchased are not available in the Procurement Service of the
Department of Budget and Management (DBM-PS). Common-use supplies not
available in the DBM-PS purchased through shopping should be supported with
a certification from the DBM-PS that such items are not available in their stock.
 Strictly observe the pertinent provisions of R.A. No 9184 and its IRR, especially
on the conduct of public bidding for the acquisition of goods and services
exceeding ₱100,000.00 and on the prohibition on splitting of contracts in order
to avail of the most advantageous price for all procurement transactions.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• Instruct the Municipal Engineer and Bids and Awards Committee to expedite the
implementation of planned infrastructure projects and procurement of
equipment necessary to mitigate the disaster risk related to such projects.
Direct the LDRRMO to incorporate in planning for the utilization of LDRRM Fund
the savings from completed Capital Outlay in the LDRRM Investment Plan.
• Direct the Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Accountant and LDRRMO to
charge only eligible items against the fund.
• Require the LDRRMO to prepare and submit to the local sanggunian through the
Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council and Local
Development Council the annual LDRRMF Investment Plan specifying in detail
the list of programs, activities and projects which include under a separate
caption those projects and programs charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of
previous years, and implement such projects at the intended period of its
implementation. Require the specification in the plan as to quarter on which
projects are to be implemented. Submit regularly or within the prescribed period
the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF to the Commission and
maintain the prescribed subsidiary ledgers for transfers of the agency’s
unutilized LDRRMF to the special trust fund by year of transfer.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
 Require the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to justify the delayed
implementation of projects listed in the 20% Development Plan, including
continuing projects, despite the availability of funds. Likewise, we
recommended that the Municipal Development Council, headed by the Municipal
Mayor, to evaluate and study the feasibility of the projects especially those with
continuing appropriations to ensure delivery of its intended services to the
public.
 Direct the Municipal Engineer to cause the immediate implementation of the
projects that had been allocated funds in order to fully attain the purpose and
intent for establishing the fund at the soonest possible time, thereby, maximizing
59
the real value of the money on hand.
 Ensure that only programs and projects geared towards the attainment of socio-
economic development and environmental management outcomes that partake
the nature of investment or capital expenditures, as mandated by law, will be
given allocation under the 20% Development Fund.
 Instruct the Municipal Accountant to draw the Journal Entry Vouchers to effect
the necessary adjustments in the PPE accounts for fair presentation of the
Statement of Financial Position.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor:
 Coordinate with the Solid Waste Management Board (SWMB) and prioritize the
creation of Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Office (MENRO).
Strictly impose the “no segregation, no collection policy”, especially at the
barangay level.
 Prioritize and expedite the establishment of an operational MRF in every
barangay or cluster of barangays in the Municipality.
 Enjoin Municipal officials to consider developing concrete programs and
projects that are supportive of proper solid waste management and reduction of
waste at source, e.g. reusing, reducing and recycling of non-biodegradable
materials. Conduct information and education campaigns on the hazards of
open burning and strictly monitor compliance thereof.

1.6 We recommended that Management direct the Municipal
Treasurer to exercise the diligence of a good father of a family in
monitoring the remittances of collections of all revenue collectors to
preclude misappropriation of the meager resources of the
government, otherwise he shall be jointly and solidarily liable with
them for any loss of government funds under his control.

2.10 We recommended that the concerned officials submit regularly
thru the Municipal Accountant to the Office of the Auditor, within ten
(10) days of the following month the Monthly Report of Fuel
Consumption pursuant to Section 361 (g), Vol. II, GAAM and COA
Circular No. 77-61. Second, Management refrains from procuring
petroleum products to illegitimate suppliers and adhere the
provisions of RA 9184. Lastly, strictly adhere to the provisions
stated under Section 4 (6) PD 1445 to avoid disallowance of the
transactions.

3.11 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the
MDRRMO to submit the required report/s pursuant to RA 10121
and COA Circular No. 2012-02 otherwise, the allowable sanctions
authorized by law shall be implemented. Furthermore, we
recommended that management strictly adhere to the provisions
under RA 10121 in utilization of said fund.

4.10 We recommended that the Municipality (a) stop the practice of
splitting contracts to justify the use of either shopping method or
small value procurement and use the alternative methods of
procurements only as provided under Rule XVI of the Revised
IRR, R.A. 9184, and (b) conduct public bidding on all
procurements of the municipality and ensure that the government
obtain the lowest and most advantageous price.

5.5 We recommended that management require the Municipal
Treasurer to remit the share of the respective barangays from real
property taxes within five days after the end of each quarter in
order that barangay constituents could use the amount at the
earliest possible time.

6.12 We recommended that the Municipality cause the remittance of
premiums contributions and loan repayments due to GSIS,
Philhealth and Pag-ibig after reconciliation and determination by
the Accountant of the correct amount to avoid further penalties as
stated in above provisions of the laws and to ensure the availment
of benefits by the officers and personnel members.

7.10 We recommended that the Accountant strongly demand the
immediate liquidation of fund transfer/financial assistance granted
to various barangays by setting specific timelines for the
liquidation. Further the Accountant should not release the fund
transfers unless the MOA and other supporting documents are
submitted by the concerned barangays.

8.7 We recommended that Management must implement the creation
of an inventory committee to conduct physical inventory taking of
the PPEs and shall prepare and reconcile inventory report to
Accounting Unit, and to instruct the General Service Office and
Accounting Office to exert extra effort in reconciling their recorded
PPEs religiously to come up with the accurate Property, Plant and
Equipment account balance.

9.7 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the
Municipal Accountant to strictly monitor and ensure that the
monthly submission of accounts, financial records and required
reports to the Auditor’s Office is within the prescribed period as
stated in the aforementioned laws, rules and regulations to
facilitate timely review and evaluation and to avoid possible
disallowance of transactions.

10.6 We recommended for Management to be conscious of the legal
deadline of the submission of the year-end reports as its non-
submission affects the audit opinion to be rendered on the said
reports.

11.5 We recommended that Management strictly enforce for the
liquidation of the cash advances and to regulate the granting of
additional cash advances especially to those who still have unsettled
cash advances.

observations.managementAction: Continously exerting efforts to reconcile the accounts
observations.reason: The Accounting Office is still in the process of reconciling the accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, together with the other concerned Municipal Officials, 
(a) adopt a systematic policy congruent with regulations in the flow of documents that will capture the faithful representation of accounting events and transactions on inventories and related accounts; and 


(b) facilitate a training to orient employees in charge of inventories on the rules and regulations in the management of inventory specifically on the maintenance of the required inventory records and on the prompt submission of inventory reports; and 
(c) maintain required inventory records.


Further, we recommended that the Accounting Office strictly comply with the policies on the proper reporting and recording of inventories pursuant to Manual on NGAS for LGUs.

Moreover, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) create a committee composed of personnel with the necessary competencies and determination to conduct the physical count of inventories and perform reconciliation efforts for the timely submission of the required report thereon pursuant to Manual on NGAS for LGUs.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant agreed to take appropriate action in coordination with other concerned Municipal Officials.

Identify the employee responsible for inventories and facilitate proper training to capacitate the personnel to effectively handle the tasks.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the LCE 
(a) create an Inventory and Appraisal Committee who will be tasked to conduct the physical inventory of the Municipality’s properties, perform reconciliation efforts for the timely submission of the required report thereon pursuant to Manual on NGAS; and 
(b) require the maintenance and keeping of the required property records pursuant to the Manual on NGAs for LGUs. 

Further, we recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Assessor to make a representation with the Registry of Deeds 
(a) for the titling of the unrecognized parcels of land owned by the Municipality; and 
(b) to secure documents on the transfer/donation of parcels of land made to GPSAT to be used as basis in the de-recognition of the same in the books,


Furthermore, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant 
(a) coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Engineer and Municipal Assessor for the proper recognition of infrastructure assets in the books; and 
(b) make the necessary adjusting entries corresponding to the resolved issues.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Assessor informed the Audit Team that some of the parcels of land which are not recognized in the books pertain to creeks and other landforms which do not require Transfer Certificate of Titles. She also added that further verification of the parcels of land will be made to ensure the ownership of the Municipality.

Meanwhile, the Municipal Accountant mentioned that necessary actions were already undertaken on the de-recognition of the donated land to Gonzalo Puyat School of Arts and Trade (GPSAT). 

Lastly, the Municipal Engineer asserted that difficulty may be encountered in the conduct of inventory of road networks and other infrastructure assets because of the lack of budget and manpower.
During the exit conference, management agreed to comply with the audit recommendations.
Management stated that they will comply with the recommendations this 2018.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the BAC 
(a) submit an explanation on the deficiencies noted for the evaluation of the Audit Team as basis for the next audit action; 
(b) submit the lacking documentary requirements; and 
(c) henceforth, strictly adhere to the laws, rules and regulations governing the procurement of goods and infrastructure projects pursuant to RIRR of RA No. 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: During the exit audit conference, the Municipal Assessor who is in-charge of organizing/sorting the bidding documents explained that the noted lacking documents were prepared together with other bidding documents, but for unknown reasons, were not attached to the file submitted to the Audit Team. Nonetheless, the said documents will be provided to the Audit Team.

According to the MDRRM Officer, the discrepancy on the amount of the LDRRMF reflected on the utilization report and Budget Office’s records was an honest mistake. Changes on the amount of LDRRMF were unintentionally not incorporated on the LDRRMF Investment Plan. He also added that the discrepancy was already settled between the offices concerned.

With regard to the accounting and reporting of in-kind donations, the MDRRM Officer commented that, although the required forms were not accomplished and reporting was not done, the documents such as DTRs and relief distribution forms were intact and submitted to the Audit Team. He further added that, henceforth, his Office will comply with the guidelines.

In relation to the observations noted on the Evacuation Center, the LCE has already instructed the Municipal Engineer to make a representation with the Contractor, to make the necessary repair and rehabilitation of the structure.

Lastly, the MDRRM Officer commented that the BAC was already informed about the comments and recommendation and the latter committed to adhere with the standards set forth by RA No. 9184.
The concerned Municipal officials particularly the MPDC assured us of the Municipality’s compliance with the audit recommendations.
Recommendations were well taken by the GFPS who agreed to comply with the recommendations.

The MCPC focal person explained that LCPC related programs and projects were implemented during the year, however, these were not included in the accomplishment report because of the lack of coordination between concerned Offices. Nevertheless, Management assured us that starting CY 2018, they will monitor the implementation of PPAs under the LCPC Fund.

The LCE commented that the audit findings were already taken up by the management. Accordingly, a memorandum order was already issued regarding the preparation of accomplishment report and evaluation of the JO personnel’s performance will be evaluated. There will be a revision of the JO personnel’s contracts.

With regard to the continuous renewal of their employment, the JO personnel were being hired under Contract of Service because the Municipality’s PS services budget was limited.

As to the hired consultant, his duties were actually on preparing correspondence and reports and not on rendering highly technical consultancy services hence, the management will consider a revision of the contract.
Management assured the Audit Team that appropriate actions are to be undertaken relative to the audit recommendations.

The Local Chief Executive gave assurance that appropriate action is being pursued in order to finally resolve the issue regarding the reconciliation of records/accounts. He also informed the Audit Team that with the use of GSIS E-Billing and Collection System (EBCS), GSIS is assured that the LGU is current in its remittance of premiums and amortization payments thus, not jeopardizing the entitlement of every employee in any of the benefits accruing them.
Management assured the Audit Team that appropriate actions are to be undertaken relative to the audit recommendations.
The concerned municipal officials had already complied with the above noted audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: For the deficiencies noted, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive, through the Municipal Development Council, efficiently and effectively plan the limited 20% DF for long-term, medium-term and annual socio-economic development PPAs with high economic development impact that are harmonized with national development plans, goals and strategies in order to optimize the utilization of resources.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer said that the concerned contractors were already informed about the defects and repairs to the noted defects will be performed.
The officials of the LSB, through their representative stated that they will comply with the recommendations this 2018.
Management reacted favorably on the audit recommendations and assured us that appropriate actions are to be undertaken.

The GSO conducted partial inventory-taking of items, and the RPCPPE was submitted to the Audit Team. However, results were not reconciled with the accounting records. The Municipal Accountant and the GSO/Property Officer are still in the process of preparing updated PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, to facilitate the reconciliation of both records. Depreciation for property, plant and equipment totaling ₱32,806,578.04 was not yet provided. Guidelines for determining the useful life of Infrastructure Assets was still not formulated by the concerned officials. Inventory of local road assets was not yet completed and the reporting guidelines for local roads asset management systems was still not complied with.
observations.reason: Lack of dedicated staff and difficulty in identifying property tags.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
1. Municipal Treasurer to send written notice to the payees for the existence of stale checks for replacement, if applicable, and comply with the procedures in the handling of stale/spoiled checks as stated in Section 59 of the NGAS Manual Volume III; and,
2. Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct balance of the Cash in Bank – Local Currency Current Account.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile with the Municipal Treasurer the ₱9,099.21 unreconciled balance between the General Ledgers and the Cashbooks and prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct balances of the Cash in Bank – Local Currency Current and Cash in Bank – Time Deposit accounts to fairly present municipality’s financial statements as of December 31, 2017.
observations.managementAction: Written notice was not sent to payees for the existence of checks. The necessary adjustments were made in the records of the concerned units. The necessary adjusting entries were drawn to reflect the correct balance of the Cash in Bank – Local Currency Current Account.
observations.reason: The matter was not prioritized by the concerned units.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries on the noted observation to reflect the correct financial performance of the municipality and observe the proper use of the Revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units (LGUs).
observations.managementAction: The necessary adjusting journal entry/voucher was drawn to reflect the necessary adjustments in the books of the municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality comply with the previously cited auditing and accounting laws and rules and submit the disbursement voucher and supporting documents to the auditor within the prescribed period to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2018, the disbursement vouchers were submitted to the Audit Team for evaluation.
observations.reason: The accounting laws and rules were observed and the supporting schedules were submitted for evaluation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality strictly comply with the provisions of Section 325(a) of Republic Act No. 7160 or the Local Government Code of 1991. The Municipality shall operate within the budgetary constraint and ensure that the local funds are allocated adequately for development projects and for the delivery of local basic services. We likewise recommended that the Municipality uncover possible potentials for income generating enterprises in order to boost the year-end income from regular sources to satisfy fully its personnel services expenditures so as to avoid incurrence of similar audit observation in the future.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2018, the Municipality spent ₱44,964,058.67 for PS during CY 2018, or 52.32% of the realized income from regular sources in CY 2016 amounting to ₱85,927,080.16. The Municipality monitored the personal services budget in accordance with the 55% PS limitation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality observe strictly the provisions of Republic Act No. 10121, rules and regulations of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and provision of Section 305(a) of Republic Act No. 7160. Allocate the required five percent (5%) of the estimated revenue from regular sources for Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF); transfer the unexpended/unobligated balance of Quick Response Fund and Local Disaster Risk Management Fund to the Trust Fund Books and refrain from fund utilization which was not provided in the risk reduction and management fund budget; submit regularly or within the prescribed period the subsidiary ledgers, Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF to the Commission and maintain the prescribed subsidiary ledgers for transfers of the agency’s unutilized LDRRMF to the special trust fund by year of transfer.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal officials committed to allocate the required percentage and submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF. The necessary adjustments were to be recorded and subsidiary ledgers maintained.
observations.reason: Lack of dedicated staff and delay in preparing required subsidiary schedules.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Designated Personnel Officer to ensure that claims are properly supported with the required documents. Refrain from charging the wages of job order workers to Personal Services and instead to Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (MOOE).
observations.managementAction: Wages of job order workers for CY 2018 were charged to the Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality of San Luis ensure effective utilization of the 20% Development Fund. The Municipal Mayor and other concerned municipal officials ensure that the 20% Development Fund is used for projects that contribute to desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes, comply with Section 305(a) of R.A. 7160 and Article 410 of its Implementing Rules and Regulations, and ensure projects are included in the Annual Investment Program for consideration and monitoring.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Planning and Development and Engineering offices will prioritize the implementation of remaining projects. Some projects were implemented and others were carried as continuing appropriations.
observations.reason: Lack of staff in Engineering Department and delay in preparation of Program of Work.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality strictly comply with Section 43 of R.A. 9710 and Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2016-01 requiring allocation of at least five percent (5%) for GAD and submission of GAD plans and accomplishment reports to the concerned offices.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality increased the allocation for gender and development and submitted required GAD PPAs and Accomplishment Report which were approved by the concerned agencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator submit prioritized projects for consideration by the concerned national government agencies in order to serve the intent of the funds and immediately address the needs of the constituents. The Municipal Accountant should correctly account trust receipts from national government agencies by recording it in the Trust Fund Books of the municipality; correctly record completed and ongoing infrastructure projects and abide by the terms in the utilization of transferred funds that unutilized funds shall be returned to the providing agency.
observations.managementAction: The necessary journal entries were taken up in the books of the municipality to correct the misclassification. The concerned units were to ensure future proper recording and recognition.
observations.reason: The concerned officials committed to incorporate the trust receipts in the AIP in the next budget year; delayed actions due to coordination with national agencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor coordinate with the Solid Waste Management Board (SWMB) and prioritize the creation of Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Office (MENRO), strictly impose the 'no segregation, no collection' policy especially at barangay level; prioritize and expedite establishment of an operational MRF in every barangay or cluster of barangays; and develop programs supporting proper solid waste management including information and education campaigns on hazards of open burning.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality completed the safe closure and rehabilitation of the open dumpsite at Barangay Tejero in March 2019. Purchase of lot for sanitary landfill was completed during CY 2018. MENRO was not yet fully established during CY 2018.
observations.reason: Lack of personnel and budget to fully operationalize MENRO and MRFs; delay in implementation of sanitary landfill.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality prepare and maintain complete subsidiary ledgers for special accounts and prepare and submit the separate statement of financial statements as required in Section 313 of RA 7160 and NGAS Manual for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality maintained subsidiary ledgers and provided separate statements for the market and hospital. The concerned units prepared the required schedules and statements.
observations.reason: Delay in consolidation of all subsidiary ledgers; some schedules still pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality carefully consider utilization of appropriated funds and increase the utilization of its appropriations against actual funds utilization; uncover potential income generating enterprises to boost year-end income from regular sources and improve service delivery.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality increased the utilization of its appropriations. The Municipality monitored specific programs and projects and implemented several priority projects in CY 2018.
observations.reason: Delay in preparation and approval of the annual budget affected timely implementation of projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant comply with the submission of the year-end financial statements and supporting schedules and submit to the Audit Team for audit.
observations.managementAction: The financial statements and supporting schedules particularly of the 26 barangays were submitted to the Audit Team for evaluation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality comply with Joint Circular No. 2015-01 of the Commission on Audit, Department of Budget and Management, Department of the Interior and Local Government, Department of National Defense and others on the use and management of Confidential Funds and that the Municipal Peace and Order Council formulate the three-year Peace and Order and Public Safety (POPS) Plan to be provided with specific appropriation.
observations.managementAction: The disbursement for Confidential Expenses was made in CY 2018. The Municipal Peace and Order Council (MPOC) shall formulate the three-year Peace and Order and Public Safety Plan.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality formulate implementing guidelines and clear-cut policies on the implementation of the Iskolar ng Bayan Program specifying the qualifications of scholars, composition of Scholarship Board/Committee, eligibility requirements, family income levels, obligations of grantees, breaches and sanctions to ensure effective program implementation.
observations.managementAction: Guidelines stating the qualifications of scholars were formulated by the Municipality. The Scholarship Program policies were established and being implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant present fairly and accurately the Municipality’s cash flow statement and the reconciliation of the budget actual amounts with the statement of financial performance pursuant to Sections 15, 21 and 27 of PPSAS I - Presentation of Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: Journal Entry was drawn to reflect the necessary adjustments and reconciliation was effected.

observations.recommendation:
We remarked favorably on the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer’s efforts in complying with the applicable tax laws using the appropriate tax rates in the purchase of goods or services; withholding taxes from employees’ compensation; and remitting in full all the taxes withheld within the prescribed period. 


However, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct all concerned Municipal Officials and employees to discontinue the practice of reimbursement for the purchase of goods and services so that amount of tax can be withheld for every transaction and the same can be remitted to BIR on the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the General Services Office maintains property cards for easy verification of existing properties and the conduct of the inventory taking would be lot easier too.

Require the Accountant also to prepare the adjusting entries on the misclassified properties.

We recommend that management directs the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the reciprocal accounts and effect adjustments to reflect the correct account balances and present reliable data pursuant to Section 111(2) of Presidential Decree No. 1445.
Observe the thresholds in the procurement of goods and implementation of infrastructure projects. Avoid splitting the requisition to evade the conduct of public bidding.
We recommend strict compliance with Section 34 of the IRR-A of RA 9184 so bona fide bidder could be awarded the contract.
We recommend that management requires the suppliers, manufacturers and distributors to establish warranty deposit in accordance with Section 62.1 of Revised IRR of R.A. 9184.
We recommend strict compliance with the above issuances by submitting perfected contracts/purchase orders and inspection reports within the time frame provided by regulations.

We recommend that Management strictly follow the fundamental principles in governing financial affairs, transactions and operations of the local government unit with emphasis on Section 305 (a) of the Local Government Code.

Further, to be cautious in disbursing funds without the support of an appropriation to preclude disallowance.

We recommend that the Local Officials prioritize the allocation of budget for carrying out the programs, projects and activities in the approved Solid Waste Management Plan. Further, informed the constituents of the importance of waste management through IEC because unless its importance is acknowledged by all sectors, waste management efforts will not progress to further heights.

We recommend that LDRRMC includes in the plan only those considered disaster risk management related activities and to observed the proper utilization of the LDRRM Fund to preclude future audit disallowance.

We recommend that Management, through the local finance committee, take the necessary safety nets relative to availing credit facilities to preclude occurrence of needing to allocate a lion’s share of their Internal Revenue Allotment to repay loans leaving little funds for the delivery of essential public services.

Further, refrain from disbursing funds without the support of an appropriation to preclude disallowance.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive (a) instruct the Municipal Accountant to settle and record all reconciling items in the bank reconciliation statements immediately in order to reflect the accurate balance of Cash in Bank accounts; (b) continue to exert efforts to obtain the necessary documents to resolve the issues on the unidentified reconciling items; and (c) determine the causes of the discrepancies between the subsidiary ledgers and the cash books for subsequent appropriate action.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant and the property custodian, as supervised by the Municipal Treasurer, to resolve/settle the unreconciled difference in their property records.

We reiterated our previous year's recommendation and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive a) ensure strict compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 on the granting, utilization, and liquidation of cash advances; (b) require the accountable officers to liquidate the overdue cash advances; (c) withhold the salary of personnel who unjustifiably fails to settle his cash advances within the prescribed period; and (d) file appropriate legal action, if warranted against those who have failed to liquidate the overdue cash advances after exhausting appropriate means, including those who are no longer in the government service.

In view of the material discrepancy of the PPE accounts and continuous
failure of the LGU to correct such deficiencies, we recommended that the LGU
consider the following:
1. The Inventory Team conduct complete inventory-taking of all the
LGU’s properties and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of
Property, Plant and Equipment to be submitted to the Auditor
concerned not later than January 31 of each year;
2. The Municipal Accountant and the GSO/Property Officer maintain complete
and updated PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, to facilitate
the reconciliation of both records;
3. The Municipal Accountant provide depreciation to property, plant and
equipment totaling P32,806,578.04 to fairly present the municipality’s
financial statements as of December 31, 2017 and review the reported
Property, Plant and Equipment in view of the discrepancy of the PPE
reported expenses in the Cash Flow Statement and the total additions to
the property, plant and equipment in CY 2017 of P16,974,693.00; and
4. Immediately comply with the accounting and reporting guidelines provided in
COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015 to fairly present the
LGU’s financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile with the
Municipal Treasurer the ₱9,099.21 unreconciled balance between the General
Ledgers and the Cashbooks and prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reflect
the correct balances of the Cash in Bank – Local Currency Current and Cash in
Bank – Time Deposit accounts to fairly present municipality’s financial
statements as of December 31, 2017.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries on the noted observation to
reflect the correct financial performance of the Municipality and observe the proper
use of the Revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units (LGUs).

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly comply with the
previously cited auditing and accounting laws and rules and regulations. Submit the
disbursement voucher and supporting documents to the auditor within the
prescribed period to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review and revise Note 25
of the Notes to Financial Statements of the financial statements of the
Municipality of San Luis for CY 2017 and effect necessary corrections to show the
correct amounts in each elements of Note 25 or Reconciliation of Net Cash Flows
from Operating Activities to Surplus/(Deficit) CY 2017.

We recommended that the Municipality observe strictly the provisions of
Section 325(a) of Republic Act No. 7160 or the Local Government Code of
1991. The Municipality shall operate within the budgetary constraint of the
PS limitation and ensure that the local funds are allocated adequately for
development projects and for the delivery of local basic services. We likewise
recommended that the Municipality uncover possible potentials for income
generating enterprises in order to boost the year-end annual income from
regular sources to satisfy fully its personnel services expenditures so as to
avoid incurrence of similar audit observation in the future.

We recommended that the Municipality observe strictly the provisions of
Republic Act No. 10121, rules and regulations of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and
provision of Section 305(a) of Republic Act No. 7160. Allocate the required
five percent (5%) of the estimated revenue from regular sources for Local
Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF); transfer the
unexpended/unobligated balance of Quick Response Fund and Local Disaster
Risk Reduction Management Fund (LDRRMF) – Maintenance and Other
Operating Expenses at the end of the year; refrain from fund utilization which was
not provided in the risk reduction and management fund budget; submit regularly
or within the prescribed period the Report on Sources and Utilization of
LDRRMF to the Commission and maintain the prescribed subsidiary ledgers for
transfers of the agency’s unutilized LDRRMF to the special trust fund by year of
transfer.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant and the Designated Personnel Officer to ensure that claims are properly
supported with the required documents as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2012-
001 dated June 14, 2012. Refrain from charging the wages of job order workers to
Personal Services and instead to Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses
(MOOE) pursuant to CSC Resolution No. 062254.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and other concerned municipal
officials ensure effective utilization of the 20% Development Fund which is
primarily intended to fund projects that shall contribute to the attainment of
desirable socio-economic development and environmental management
outcomes. We further recommended the strict observance of Section 305(a) of
Republic Act No. 7160 and Article 410 of its Implementing Rules and
Regulations. Non-observance of Section 305(a) is a violation of appropriation
ordinance while of Article 410 is against the local government code of 1991 and
any project implemented becomes questionable if it was not included among the
priorities.

We recommended that the Municipality strictly comply with Section 43 of
Republic Act No. 9710 or “The Magna Carta of Women allocating at least five
(5) percent of its total appropriation for gender and development. We further
recommended stringent compliance with the provision of Section 5(2) of the Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2016-01 dated January 12, 2016 of the Philippine
Commission on Women, Department of the Interior and Local Government,
Department of Budget and Management and National Economic and
Development Authority that the municipality shall submit their Annual Gender
and Development (GAD) Plan and Budget (GPB) to the Provincial Planning
and Development Office (PPDO) of the Provincial Government to ensure the
alignment of the municipal GAD PPAs with the priorities of the province and
then to the DILG Provincial Office for its review and endorsement to the
concerned LGU for incorporation in their annual budget to be enacted by her
Local Sanggunian.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, as the Chairperson of the
Local Council for the Protection of Children (LCPC), direct the members of the
Council to prepare Local Development Plan for Children and Local Investment
Plan for Children and allocate one percent (1%) of Internal Revenue Allotment
(IRA) for the strengthening and implementation of the programs of the Local
Council for the Protection of Children (LCPC) pursuant to Section 15 of R.A.
No. 9344.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor with the assistance of the
Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator submit prioritized projects
identified by the Municipality in the Annual Investment Program for
consideration in the selection of projects by the concerned national
government agencies in order to serve the intent of the funds and to
immediately address the needs of the constituents.

We further recommended that Municipal Accountant properly account trust
receipts from national government agencies by recording it in the Trust Fund Books
of the municipality; correctly record completed and ongoing infrastructure
projects and abide by the terms in the utilization of transferred funds that
unutilized funds shall be returned immediately to the providing agency.

We recommended that the Municipal Government of San Luis exert more
effort to strictly comply with the provisions of Republic Act No. 9003. The
Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Office (MENRO) should
ensure that the public has high level of awareness on R.A 9003 thru
distribution of flyers/leaflets and conducting campaigns and seminars in every
barangay which in effect shall educate the public in every way possible
regarding the importance of solid waste management, the benefits of
complying with the said law, the detrimental effect of garbage to one’s health,
the advantages and disadvantages of having and not having a MRF and legal
implications of non-compliance with the Act. We also enjoined the offices
concerned to expedite the procurement of lot in order to commence the
establishment of sanitary landfill in the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipality duly consider the instructive
guidelines on the creation or establishment of the local economic enterprise
provided in the Manual on Setting Up and Operation of Local Economic
Enterprises for Local Government Units 2016. It particularly emphasizes the
effective local economic enterprise or the enterprise that contributes to the
quality service delivery goals of the LGU. As such, it must be designed, set
up, operated, and managed in accordance with a well-prepared feasibility
study and a detailed business plan.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide and maintain a
complete set of subsidiary ledgers for special accounts and prepare and submit
financial statements as required in Section 313 of RA 7160 and Sections 107 and
110 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipality carefully consider the noted
percentages of actual funds utilization against appropriated funds. These are
indicators of LGU’s effectiveness and efficiency on the delivery of local public
basic services and facilities.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant fully comply with the
aforementioned law, rules and regulations of government accounting and auditing
on the submission of the year-end financial statements particularly of the 26
barangays of the municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the honorable members of
the Sanggunian include among its top priorities the construction of a building for
the safekeeping and accommodation of the increasing volume of documents
generated by the various departments/offices of the Municipality, particularly
those for submission to the audit team, with consideration on the preservation
period of ten (10) years as mandated by law.

We recommended that the Municipality strictly adhere to the provisions of
the Joint Circular No. 2015-01 of Commission on Audit, Department of Budget and
Management, Department of the Interior and Local Government, Governance
Commission for GOCCs and Department of National Defense dated January 8,
2015. The Municipal Peace and Order Council (MPOC) shall formulate the three
- year Peace and Order and Public Safety (POPS) Plan that shall contain the Peace
and Order Programs (POP) of the LGU that will be provided with specific
appropriation in its annual budget on which the amount of the Confidential Fund
shall be based. Likewise, the mode of utilization of Confidential Fund is through
cash advance and the Local Chief Executive shall designate the Special Disbursing
Officer (SDO) for this fund except if he himself is the SDO.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive formulate implementing
guidelines and clear-cut policies on the implementation of the Iskolar ng Bayan
Program, specifying among others, the creation and composition of the
Scholarship Board/Committee, the eligibility requirements, qualifying maximum
family income level of applicants to ensure that only poor students, as determined
under Republic Act 8425, otherwise known as the Social Reform and Poverty
Alleviation Act dated 11 December 1997, to avail of the scholarship, in addition
to other requirements such as the minimum grade, the specific degree courses,
the obligations of the grantees, the circumstances considered as breach or default
and the sanctions to be imposed in such cases for the effective and efficient
implementation of the program.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant present fairly and
accurately the Municipality’s cash flow statement and the reconciliation of the
budget actual amounts with the statement of financial performance pursuant to
Sections 15, 21 and 27 of Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards I –
Presentation of Financial Statements.
We recommended that Management direct all officers and employees to strictly adhere with rules and regulations on the liquidation of cash advances.
We recommended that management direct the General Services Office to conduct the inventory taking and to prepare the property cards for easy verification of the properties.
We recommended that management strictly adhere to the provisions of R.A. 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations in the procurement of goods to preclude same observation in the future.

We recommended to the management that the personnel in-charge in the segregation and submission of the accounts to be mindful of the due dates of the submission of accounts for the timely review and evaluation of such.
We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant and the concerned employees to submit the lacking documents as specified in the attached schedule.
We recommended that the LGU appropriate funds, of what is required by law, for the strengthening and implementation of programs of the LCPC.

We recommended that management strictly comply with the provision of law particularly in availing credit or indebtedness and be cognizant of the conditions as to when such indebtedness can be applied to.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management should (1) demand for the immediate settlement of outstanding advances; (2) strictly adhere with the governing laws, rules and regulations on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances; and (3) to refrain from granting additional cash advances to accountable officers unless his/her previous cash advance is liquidated or a proper accounting thereof has already been made.

We recommend that the Municipal Engineer furnish the Accountant with advance copies of the Certificate of Completion and Acceptance of projects even before payment is being processed. The Accountant shall transfer and properly record the completed and on-going projects to the appropriate agency asset in order to present fairly the value of the CIP accounts in the financial statements.

We recommend that management prepares subsidiary ledger and/or schedules of all the accounts including the details of the items listed therein on a timely basis to establish the existence, accuracy and reliability of the recorded amounts.
We recommend that the misclassified accounts be reclassified/adjusted by the Municipal Accountant in conformity with the standard chart of accounts for fair presentation of the financial statements.

We recommend strict compliance with the aforementioned laws, rules and regulations on all government procurement. Stop the practice of reimbursements for purchase of goods and services and on account of the agency’s programs and activities. Lastly, ensure that all payments must be made by check except when the circumstances call for impracticability, difficulty and impossibility of payments through checks.

We recommend that Management directs the Bids and Awards Committee to strictly complies with the provision on mandatory posting of procurement opportunities of the LGU and be reminded that EO 662, as amended by EO 662-A expressly provides that non-compliance with posting obligations shall render the accountable officials liable for dereliction of duty.

We therefore recommend that the personnel in-charge in the segregation and submission of the accounts to be mindful of the due dates of the submission of accounts for the timely review and evaluation of such.

We recommend that management strengthen the linkage of planning and budgeting in making the budget more credible. Also, proper recording of transactions should at all times be adhered to in making the financial reports reliable.

We recommend that the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (LDRRMC), as fund in-charge, strictly complies the procedures in the utilization of the QRF and the LDRRMF as a whole to preclude incurrence of suspension and disallowance in the future.

We recommend that Management ensure that the 20% Development Fund be optimally utilized to help achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes as it is the responsibility of every local chief executive to do so and to remind management that utilizing the fund for any purpose beyond those expressly prescribed by law or public policy could be subject to sanctions provided under the Local Government Code.

Further, avoid charging ineligible expenditures to the 20% DF to preclude incurrence of future disallowance.

We recommend that the Local School Board, as mandated in RA 5447, properly determine the needs of the schools within its jurisdiction, so that the real purpose of the creation of the Fund could be fully achieved. Stop charging ineligible expenses to the SEF and follow strictly Section 272 of RA 7160 on the proper utilization of the fund.

We recommended and management agreed that the (a) Municipal Accountant (i) to exert efforts to obtain the necessary documents to support the unidentified reconciling items to effect the needed adjustments; and (ii) henceforth, ensure that BRSs are prepared within the prescribed period and the necessary adjustments are effected; and (b) personnel from the Accounting Office in-charge of preparation of BRSs ensures that all identified reconciling items are analyzed, fully documented and recorded in the books.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (i) stop the practice of granting new cash advances without the liquidation of previous ones; (ii) require the concerned personnel to liquidate their cash advances as soon as the purpose thereof had been accomplished; (iii) require liquidation of  a) salaries, wages, etc. within five (5) days after each fifteen (day)/end of the month pay period ; b) and for official travel  within (60) days after return to the Philippines in the case of foreign travel or within thirty (30) days after return to his permanent official station in the case of local travel as provided for in EO No. 248 and COA Circular No. 96-004; c) petty operating expenses and field operating expenses within 20 days after the end of the year and subject to replenishment as frequently as necessary during the year; (iv) demand for the liquidation of all cash advances as required under COA Circular No. 97-002, and COA Memorandum No. 2014-014 and (v) exercise  withholding of salary and implement the instruction of other sanctions as provided for under paragraphs 9.2 and 9.3 of  COA Circular  No. 97-002 to accountable officers, if necessary.

Issued and sent demand letter and require immediate liquidation of outstanding cash advances within the prescribed period.

Continously exerting efforts to demand liquidation of cash advances from accountable officers concerned

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MSWDO in coordination with the Office of the Senior Citizens conduct the necessary verification, identification, and monitoring/updating of beneficiaries to ensure that the release of social pension is made only to eligible and deserving senior citizens in an efficient manner.
observations.managementAction:
Management mentioned that the list of senior citizens and payroll were provided by the DSWD and the National Housing Targeting System (NHTS), and the actual period of census was not known to them hence, they could not conduct verification or validation.

 However, the Audit Team stressed out the DSWD Administrative Order No. 3, series of 2011, which provides the procedures in identifying the qualified social pensioners, which starts with the master list to be provided by the Social Pension Management Office (SPMO), taken from the data of the DSWD NHTS-PR. The SPMO shall provide the Regional Social Pension Unit (RSPU) with a regional list, which will be segregated by the latter into city and municipal list to be provided to the OSCA C/MSWDO for verification and validation. The OSCA C/MSWDO-validated list together with the intake sheets shall be returned to the RSPU for data banking and consolidation vis-a-vis the master list.

No. 6713 and R.A. No. 3019 on conflict of interest in every transaction of the Municipality; (iii) strictly observe the provisions in RA 9184 and its IRR, particularly the conduct of competitive bidding, and posting with PhilGEPS and its website; and (iv) resort only to the alternative method of procurement in highly exceptional cases, as provided under the implementing guidelines of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction:
Management will apply the audit recommendations on future similar projects/programs.
